description: A DataRef whose optional 'format' field is JSON null. acdp-data-ref.schema.json types 'format' as a bare string ("type": "string"), NOT as "type": ["string", "null"]. Per the absent-vs-null wire convention (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1, RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.8), an absent optional DataRef field MUST be omitted, never serialized as null. A registry MUST reject a publish carrying 'format': null, and a consumer validating a retrieved body MUST reject it — in both cases as schema_violation.

expected.rationale: DataRef.format is optional and non-nullable: it is either absent or a string. null is not a valid wire value. The only conformant way to express 'no format' is to omit the key. A permissive deserializer that coerces null to None/absent (e.g. one not applying a present-vs-null guard) silently accepts non-conformant input and would also diverge on content_hash, since JCS treats an absent key and "format": null as distinct bytes. Contrast supersedes (RFC-ACDP-0002 §3.1), whose schema type is string|null and which is therefore legitimately nullable.
